title: lantana triplinervia var. minasensis
intro: Lantana triplinervia var. minasensis, also known as Brazilian shrub lantana, is a plant species within the Lantana genus, characterized by its compact growth habit and clusters of small flowers. The plant is known to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Lantana triplinervia var. minasensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas or decoctions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
